key_points: Roommate turnover is high; multiple dropouts mid-semester is common
Petersen Hall triples frequently become unstable living situations
Room change waitlist exists but process is unclear and frustrating
key_quotes: I've gone through 3 roommates now. 3. And none of them have lasted longer than a month.
I don't necessarily click with the other girl I'm rooming with... considering moving to a dorm on the east side.
If you're female and wanting to room change please let me know!

key_points: Making friends is genuinely hard, especially for transfers and older students
On Iowa! orientation events are praised as the best friend-making opportunity
Bar scene dominates; non-drinkers struggle to find social footing
key_quotes: Transfer student here! I really wanted to like this school but it just wasn't for me. It's super difficult to make friends.
I found it extremely hard to make meaningful friends. Also feel like I don't really belong in this city.
My number one best advice is to go to silent disco. Get there early, it is SO popular you will meet so many people.

key_points: Main library mac and cheese has cult following among students
Dining dollar budget runs out; students look for workarounds
Aramark contract draws student protest over food service quality
key_quotes: Does anyone either know or have access to the recipe for the main library mac and cheese? It is decadent. It is heavenly. Otherworldly, even.
I'm running out of dining dollars and I want to make it at home.
Petition to end U Iowa's relationship with Aramark...

key_points: Freshman academic struggles and isolation are widely reported
Multiple posts show students feeling lost, friendless, or overwhelmed
Community support exists but students must actively seek it out
key_quotes: It's been around 4 weeks since school started and I still haven't made any friends.
I am 20 years old... I haven't made any friends here.
I feel like giving up. If I drop I'd have to take a W but I can't decide.

key_points: Published merit scholarship calculator makes aid predictable and transparent
OOS students frequently shocked by $40k/year costs after aid
In-state tuition seen as strong value; OOS is a harder sell
key_quotes: After having used my FAFSA info and the cost calculator I am still expected to be billed right around 40k/yr.
I turned down Purdue for the University of Iowa. I'm poor lol and who can afford to pay $38,000 a year.
University of Iowa publishes merit scholarship amounts based on your stats.

key_points: Iowa Writers' Workshop is world-class, genuinely Juilliard-tier for writing
Nursing program is top-10 but non-direct-admit path is brutal and opaque
CS 1210 has reputation for being unfairly difficult even for strong coders
key_quotes: Iowa writers' workshop for creative writing would be something like getting into Juilliard for music.
Without having direct admit to the nursing program I probably wouldn't. I've seen several people not get in after their second year.
I have heard horrific things about CS 1210 at UIowa and how even those who are great at coding do terrible in the class.

key_points: DEI programs and African American Studies cut during Black History Month
New 'Center for Intellectual Freedom' seen as political and poorly attended
Parking and fee hikes generate persistent student frustration
key_quotes: Of course they announced the closure of the African American Studies major during Black History Month. Because the cruelty is the point.
We need to eliminate degrees with low enrollment to save money. Also... we need to spend millions on whatever this is.
I'm so annoyed how this school is a constant money grab.

key_points: Iowa City is walkable, vibrant, and genuinely beloved by students
Campus and city are integrated; tons of restaurants, events, culture nearby
Winter is brutal; classes almost never cancelled regardless of conditions
key_quotes: Iowa City in the summertime is the BEST time in Iowa City.
Welcome to Iowa bro. Shit is never canceled. Not even Armageddon.
When I was there they only cancelled class in extreme cold (-40 windchill) because a kid died a couple years ago.

key_points: Finance students openly worried about IB/hedge fund recruiting limitations
Engineering grads report strong personal attention and good outcomes
Creative writing and nursing grads benefit from program prestige directly
key_quotes: I'm looking into jobs in investment banking/hedge fund management and it seems my opportunities will be very limited.
I chose Iowa over Illinois/Purdue. The fit cannot be overstated. I wanted no part of a giant weed-out program.
Engineering students at Iowa are a much smaller group and treated very very well. Personal attention. They help you.

key_points: In-state is widely seen as a great deal with predictable merit aid
OOS sticker price around $40k/year surprises many out-of-state students
Parking fees, gym costs, and nickel-and-diming erode goodwill over time
key_quotes: It's at the point where it's cheaper for me to get a gym membership elsewhere on top of tuition.
My main reason I don't donate to fundraisers to UI as an alumni... is that I paid so much money to parking.
Iowa is the cheapest by a solid amount, but gave less scholarship/aid.

key_points: Big Ten football culture is a genuine social glue for many students
Iowa City itself is a hidden gem — artsy, liberal, surprisingly lively
Political climate on campus is tense under current state-level pressure
key_quotes: I go to Iowa! I'm an out of state student from Florida and I absolutely love love LOVE it here.
I hate this state.
It's weird that we had one liberal-ish university president and people protested in her lawn. Now we got hard right lunatics actively trying to turn the university into a MAGA factory.

key_points: Safety: Pedestrian Mall shootings occurred within feet of campus repeatedly
Greek Life: Documented fraternity hazing exposed by police investigation
Housing: Iowa City landlords described as predatory with illegal practices reported
key_quotes: Your kid at Iowa is much more likely to be SA'd by another college student or get injured from binge drinking than shot in the ped mall.
The weekly HawkAlerts about people being SAed in dorms might be a more pressing concern.
He changed the locks on the front door and locked everyone out... withheld mail from all tenants for months. Time to move. IC has many slum lords.

key_points: Cambus and Bionic Bus system is genuinely excellent and free for students
Iowa City summer scene: farmers market, concerts, cat cafe, drag shows
Small engineering program means real professor access, not weed-out culture
key_quotes: The Cambus is great... there's also the bionic bus that is an option. It will even take you to the mall.
Filmscene in the park, farmers market every Saturday, Friday night concert series, Summer of the Arts...
I liked the small Engineering program at Iowa. Class sizes were small after sophomore year and if you needed it, you could get lots of 1:1 instructional time.
